author: chatgpt-codex-connector
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Specify how firmware receives calibration limits**

For the likely Uno/Nano-style Arduino in this generic kit, the sketch cannot read `arm/calibration.json` from the Git repo at runtime, so this acceptance criterion is not implementable as written. After Day 7 writes new min/max values, the Python side can load them but the on-board clamp will still use defaults unless the blueprint adds a serial limit-upload handshake, generated header + reflash step, or another explicit path before pulses are emitted.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
